methods.method_name: Stalagmite Sample Preparation and Petrographic Observation
methods.description: Process for preparing stalagmite samples for petrographic observation and analysis.

steps.description: The stalagmite (sample K1-2010) was cut along its growth axis after retrieval from the cave, and polished using 120-4000 µm silicon carbide (SiC) paper.
steps.searchable_summary: cut and polish stalagmite sample using SiC paper

steps.description: Petrographic observations were performed with an optical binocular microscope.

methods.description: Procedure for dating stalagmite samples using U-series dating and isotope analysis.

steps.description: The first 10 U-series datings were carried out at the NERC Isotope Geosciences Laboratory (NIGL), British Geological Survey, Keyworth, UK.
Seven new ages were recently completed by the NIGL geochemistry laboratory and the Geochemistry Laboratory, Earth Science Department, University of Melbourne, Australia.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain new U-series ages from NIGL and University of Melbourne

steps.description: Powdered 100 to 400 mg calcite samples were collected with a dental drill from 11 levels along the growth axis of the speleothem, taking care to sample along growth horizons.
steps.searchable_summary: collect powdered calcite samples using dental drill

steps.description: Chemical separation and purification of uranium and thorium were performed following the procedures of Edwards et al. (1987) with modifications.
steps.searchable_summary: separate and purify uranium and thorium

steps.description: Data were obtained on a Thermo Neptune Plus multicollector inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometer (MC-ICP-MS) following procedures modified from Anderson et al. (2008), Heiss et al. (2012), and Hellstrom (2006).
Mass bias and SEM gain for Th measurements were corrected using an in-house 229 Th-230 Th-232 Th reference solution calibrated against CRM 112a.
steps.searchable_summary: correct mass bias and SEM gain for Th measurements

steps.description: Quoted uncertainties for activity ratios, initial 234 U / 238 U, and ages include a ca. 0.2 % uncertainty calculated from the combined 236 U / 229 Th tracer calibration uncertainty and measurement reproducibility of reference materials (HU-1, CRM 112a, in-house Th reference solution) as well as the measured isotope ratio uncertainty.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate uncertainties for activity ratios and ages

steps.description: Ages are calculated from time of analysis (2014) and also in years before 1950 with an uncertainty at the 2σ level, typically of between 500 and 1000 years.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ages from analysis time and before 1950

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing stable isotopes δ13C and δ18O in stalagmite samples.

steps.description: Samples for δ13C and δ18O measurements were drilled along the speleothem central axis using a 1 mm dental drill.
steps.searchable_summary: drill samples for δ13C and δ18O measurements

steps.description: Ethanol was used to clean the speleothem surface and drill bit prior to sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: clean speleothem surface and drill bit with ethanol

steps.description: A total of 206 samples were analysed using the Nu Carb carbonate device coupled to a Nu Perspective MS at the Vrije Universiteit Brussel with analytical uncertainties less than 0.1 % (2 s) for oxygen and 0.05 % (2 s) for carbon.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using Nu Carb device and Nu Perspective MS

steps.description: Isotopic equilibrium analyses were carried out using six recent calcite samples collected in the cave and Hendy tests (Hendy, 1971) were carried out at five different locations along the speleothem growth axis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotopic equilibrium analyses and Hendy tests

steps.description: No evidence for severe out-of-equilibrium deposition was detected along the growth axis of the stalagmite.

methods.description: Collection and analysis of water samples for δ18O measurements using CRDS technique.

steps.description: Three seepage water samples and three water pool samples from Kanaan Cave were collected for δ18O measurements in hermetically sealed glass bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples for δ18O measurements

steps.description: Measurements were performed at the Vrije Universiteit Brussel on a Picarro L2130i analyser using the cavity ring-down spectroscopy (CRDS) technique.
steps.searchable_summary: perform δ18O measurements using Picarro L2130i and CRDS

steps.description: All values are reported in per mill (‰) relative to Vienna Standard Mean Ocean Water (V-SMOW2). Analytical uncertainties (2σ) were less than 0.10 ‰.
steps.searchable_summary: report δ18O values relative to V-SMOW2 with uncertainties
